title: rubus horridus var. humifusus
intro: Rubus horridus var. humifusus, also known as the creeping blackberry, is a low-growing, thorny shrub native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its small, edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Rubus horridus var. humifusus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies for various health conditions. The plant is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, which can be used externally to treat skin conditions, wounds, and other health issues.
